title: Quality Management System Control Evidence Freshness (Art. 17)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for Art.13.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that EU AI Act Art.13 (Transparency and information) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.21.16: Quality Management System Control Evidence Freshness (Art. 17).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.21.16' running on index=qms CSV via DB Connect or sourcetype=qms:control:event — control_id, last_review, owner, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=qms` CSV via DB Connect or `sourcetype=qms:control:event` — control_id, last_review, owner
app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
spl:
index=qms sourcetype="qms:control:event" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(last_review) as lr_epoch by control_id, owner
| eval days_since=round((now()-lr_epoch)/86400,1)
| where days_since > 365 OR isnull(lr_epoch)
| sort - days_since
| table control_id, owner, days_since
description: Monitors last_review date for each QMS control_id to ensure Art. 17 quality management system remains current.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes qms and sourcetypes qms:control:event, qms:control:event`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ingest QMS export nightly; (2) map owners to RACI; (3) alert >365d; (4) attach evidence artifact hash field when available. Indexes required: `index=qms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qms:control:event`, `sourcetype=qms:control:event``.
visualization: Table (stale controls), Bar chart (days_since distribution), Single value (stale count)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=qms` CSV via DB Connect or `sourcetype=qms:control:event` — control_id, last_review, owner.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest QMS export nightly; (2) map owners to RACI; (3) alert >365d; (4) attach evidence artifact hash field when available.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=qms sourcetype="qms:control:event" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(last_review) as lr_epoch by control_id, owner
| eval days_since=round((now()-lr_epoch)/86400,1)
| where days_since > 365 OR isnull(lr_epoch)
| sort - days_since
| table control_id, owner, days_since
```

Understanding this SPL

**Quality Management System Control Evidence Freshness (Art. 17) (EU AI Act)** — Monitors last_review date for each QMS control_id to ensure Art. 17 quality management system remains current.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=qms` CSV via DB Connect or `sourcetype=qms:control:event` — control_id, last_review, owner. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: qms; **sourcetype**: qms:control:event. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=qms, sourcetype="qms:control:event", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **control_id, owner**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_since > 365 OR isnull(lr_epoch)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: control_id, days_since, lr_epoch, owner.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stale controls), Bar chart (days_since distribution), Single value (stale count)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for monitors last_review date for each qms control_id to ensure art. 17 quality management system remains current. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
